rationale: The citing paper references Wansink and Chandon (2006) only once, in a grouped introductory citation list used to illustrate that health and safety interventions can have unintended backfire effects. The paper does not single out this study for specific theoretical development, hypothesis building, mechanism borrowing, measurement use, or result comparison. Its core framework is built around political ideology and psychological reactance, so this citation functions as broad background evidence rather than a distinct input.
